result.summary: Mollie exposes a public REST API at `https://api.mollie.com/v2` and an MCP server at `https://mcp.mollie.com/mcp`; both require Mollie-issued bearer credentials, and no official GraphQL API or CLI was found.
result.description: Mollie is a European payments platform for online and in-person payment processing, including payment links, recurring billing, terminals, and platform/marketplace payment capabilities. It provides merchant and platform infrastructure for accepting, managing, and reporting on payments.

mollie_api_key.label: Mollie API key
mollie_api_key.setup: In the Mollie Dashboard, open [Developers > API keys](https://my.mollie.com/dashboard/developers/api-keys) and create or copy an API key for your account/profile. Use the test key for sandbox requests and the live key for production, as described in [Authentication](https://docs.mollie.com/reference/authentication) and [Testing](https://docs.mollie.com/reference/testing). Send it as an `Authorization: Bearer <api-key>` header.

mollie_access_token.label: Mollie OAuth access token
mollie_access_token.setup: For Connect/platform integrations, register an app in [Developers > OAuth applications](https://my.mollie.com/dashboard/developers/oauth-applications), configure its redirect URL, and complete Mollie OAuth to obtain an access token for a connected organization/account as documented in [Authentication](https://docs.mollie.com/reference/authentication). Use the resulting bearer token in `Authorization: Bearer <access-token>`. Some endpoints also require a `profileId` in the request when using OAuth, as noted in endpoint docs such as [Create session](https://docs.mollie.com/reference/create-session).

mollie_org_token.label: Mollie organization access token
mollie_org_token.setup: In the Mollie Dashboard, open [Developers > Access tokens](https://my.mollie.com/dashboard/developers/access-tokens) and create an organization access token if your integration needs organization-scoped access, as referenced in [Authentication](https://docs.mollie.com/reference/authentication). Use it as `Authorization: Bearer <organization-access-token>`. Some endpoints also require a `profileId` in the request when using organization tokens, as noted in endpoint docs such as [Create session](https://docs.mollie.com/reference/create-session).
